title: The Conditional Tense
shortSummary: Learn to express what would happen, make polite requests, give advice, and discuss hypothetical situations. The conditional is your key to sounding more sophisticated and polite in Spanish.
whyItMatters: The conditional lets you be polite (Me gustaría...), give advice (Yo que tú, haría...), and discuss hypothetical scenarios. It is essential for professional and social contexts where directness is not appropriate.
coreConcepts: Irregular conditional stems: tendría, haría, diría, podría
Giving advice: Yo que tú / En tu lugar
Expressing probability in the past: Serían las tres
